title: podman machine doesn't work on macOS Big Sur 11.6 MacBook Pro 2018 Intel i7
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

`podman machine start` seems to work but `podman` commands return error, e.g.:

```
% podman machine start
INFO[0000] waiting for clients...                       
INFO[0000] listening tcp://0.0.0.0:7777                 
INFO[0000] new connection from  to /var/folders/qh/xw68c7sn2r72tltmnnd2bbnr0000gn/T/podman/qemu_podman-machine-default.sock 
Waiting for VM ...
qemu-system-x86_64: warning: host doesn't support requested feature: CPUID.80000001H:ECX.svm [bit 2]
% podman info
Error: cannot connect to the Podman socket, please verify that Podman REST API service is running: Get "http://d/v3.3.1/libpod/_ping": dial unix ///var/folders/qh/xw68c7sn2r72tltmnnd2bbnr0000gn/T/podman-run--1/podman/podman.sock: connect: no such file or directory
```

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. `brew install podman`
2. `podman machine init`
3. `podman machine start`
4. `podman info`

**Describe the results you received:**

`podman` command doesn't work

**Describe the results you expected:**

`podman` command works

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
% podman version
Error: cannot connect to the Podman socket, please verify that Podman REST API service is running: Get "http://d/v3.3.1/libpod/_ping": dial unix ///var/folders/qh/xw68c7sn2r72tltmnnd2bbnr0000gn/T/podman-run--1/podman/podman.sock: connect: no such file or directory
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
% podman info --debug
Error: cannot connect to the Podman socket, please verify that Podman REST API service is running: Get "http://d/v3.3.1/libpod/_ping": dial unix ///var/folders/qh/xw68c7sn2r72tltmnnd2bbnr0000gn/T/podman-run--1/podman/podman.sock: connect: no such file or directory
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
% brew info podman
podman: stable 3.3.1 (bottled), HEAD
Tool for managing OCI containers and pods
https://podman.io/
/usr/local/Cellar/podman/3.3.1 (170 files, 39MB) *
  Poured from bottle on 2021-09-14 at 14:32:08
From: https://github.com/Homebrew/homebrew-core/blob/HEAD/Formula/podman.rb
License: Apache-2.0
==> Dependencies
Build: go ✔, go-md2man ✘
Required: qemu ✔
==> Options
--HEAD
	Install HEAD version
==> Caveats
zsh completions have been installed to:
  /usr/local/share/zsh/site-functions
==> Analytics
install: 10,087 (30 days), 16,666 (90 days), 42,281 (365 days)
install-on-request: 10,087 (30 days), 16,658 (90 days), 41,831 (365 days)
build-error: 0 (30 days)
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**

**Yes**/No

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

macOS Big Sur 11.5.2 MacBook Prof 2018 Intel i7

Other potentially related observations:

1. `ls` shows running (and the `CPUID.80000001H:ECX.svm` warning in `podman machine start` seems to be benign as per [issue #11421](https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/11421)):
   ```
   % podman machine ls
   NAME                     VM TYPE     CREATED            LAST UP
   podman-machine-default*  qemu        About an hour ago  Currently running
   ```
1. Listing the directory above:
   ```
   % ls -l /var/folders/qh/xw68c7sn2r72tltmnnd2bbnr0000gn/T/podman/
   total 8
   -rw-r--r--  1 kevin  staff  4 Sep 14 16:12 podman-machine-default.pid
   srwxr-xr-x  1 kevin  staff  0 Sep 14 16:12 podman-machine-default_ready.sock
   srwxr-xr-x  1 kevin  staff  0 Sep 14 16:12 qemu_podman-machine-default.sock
   srwxr-xr-x  1 kevin  staff  0 Sep 14 16:12 qmp_podman-machine-default.sock
   ```
1. Reviewed the [Troubleshooting page](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md) and didn't find anything.
1. Reviewed the [Using podman-machine on MacOS page](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/docs/tutorials/mac_experimental.md) but step 8 says "you need to know the name of the compressed file that you were given" and I don't know where that is given.
1. Detailed system information:
   ```
   % system_profiler SPHardwareDataType
   Hardware:
   
       Hardware Overview:
   
         Model Name: MacBook Pro
         Model Identifier: MacBookPro15,1
         Processor Name: 6-Core Intel Core i7
         Processor Speed: 2.6 GHz
         Number of Processors: 1
         Total Number of Cores: 6
         L2 Cache (per Core): 256 KB
         L3 Cache: 9 MB
         Hyper-Threading Technology: Enabled
         Memory: 16 GB
         System Firmware Version: 1554.140.20.0.0 (iBridge: 18.16.14759.0.1,0)
         Serial Number (system): ...
         Hardware UUID: ...
         Provisioning UDID: ...
         Activation Lock Status: Enabled
   ```

comments.body: Can you show the output of `podman system connection ls`.

@Luap99 It's blank:

```
% podman machine ls
NAME                     VM TYPE     CREATED       LAST UP
podman-machine-default*  qemu        16 hours ago  Currently running
% podman system connection ls
% 
```

The error message says it can't find the socket at `///var/folders/qh/xw68c7sn2r72tltmnnd2bbnr0000gn/T/podman-run--1/podman/podman.sock` and just to add that I looked and the following parent directory exists but is blank:

```
% ls -l /var/folders/qh/xw68c7sn2r72tltmnnd2bbnr0000gn/T/podman-run--1
% 
```

I noticed that during the `podman machine start`, it pointed to a different directory although there is no `podman.sock` there:

```
% podman machine start
[...]
INFO[0000] new connection from  to /var/folders/qh/xw68c7sn2r72tltmnnd2bbnr0000gn/T/podman/qemu_podman-machine-default.sock 
[...]

% ls -l /var/folders/qh/xw68c7sn2r72tltmnnd2bbnr0000gn/T/podman/
total 8
-rw-r--r--  1 kevin  staff  4 Sep 14 16:12 podman-machine-default.pid
srwxr-xr-x  1 kevin  staff  0 Sep 14 16:12 podman-machine-default_ready.sock
srwxr-xr-x  1 kevin  staff  0 Sep 14 16:12 qemu_podman-machine-default.sock
srwxr-xr-x  1 kevin  staff  0 Sep 14 16:12 qmp_podman-machine-default.sock
```
FYI, I just had to upgrade to macOS Big Sur 11.6, although it's still the same issue.
if you remove the machine and init a new one, does that work ?

@baude That worked, thanks!!

```
% podman machine rm
ERRO[0000] unable to find connection named "podman-machine-default" 
ERRO[0000] unable to find connection named "podman-machine-default-root" 

The following files will be deleted:

/Users/kevin/.ssh/podman-machine-default
/Users/kevin/.ssh/podman-machine-default.pub
/Users/kevin/.config/containers/podman/machine/qemu/podman-machine-default.ign
/Users/kevin/.local/share/containers/podman/machine/qemu/podman-machine-default_fedora-coreos-34.20210904.1.0-qemu.x86_64.qcow2
/Users/kevin/.config/containers/podman/machine/qemu/podman-machine-default.json


Are you sure you want to continue? [y/N] y
% podman machine ls
NAME        VM TYPE     CREATED     LAST UP
% podman machine init
Extracting compressed file
% podman machine start
INFO[0000] waiting for clients...                       
INFO[0000] listening tcp://0.0.0.0:7777                 
INFO[0000] new connection from  to /var/folders/qh/xw68c7sn2r72tltmnnd2bbnr0000gn/T/podman/qemu_podman-machine-default.sock 
Waiting for VM ...
qemu-system-x86_64: warning: host doesn't support requested feature: CPUID.80000001H:ECX.svm [bit 2]
% podman machine ls   
NAME                     VM TYPE     CREATED         LAST UP
podman-machine-default*  qemu        40 seconds ago  Currently running
% podman version
Client:
Version:      3.3.1
API Version:  3.3.1
Go Version:   go1.17
Built:        Mon Aug 30 12:15:26 2021
OS/Arch:      darwin/amd64

Server:
Version:      3.3.1
API Version:  3.3.1
Go Version:   go1.16.6
Built:        Mon Aug 30 13:46:36 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```
